fear: Being drawn into a legal proceeding that will delay his consist further and require him to appear before a justice of the peace on his own time.
name: Dolan Marsh
voice: Flat, matter-of-fact, mildly amused by the situation — the voice of a man who has seen a great deal from locomotive cabs and finds most human drama unsurprising.
secrets: He recognized the man in the wool coat because he has seen him before on the CBQ platform at the Western Springs depot — a well-dressed man who takes the early Chicago train on Thursdays.
He told the railroad bull on duty about what he saw but the bull told him to keep his nose in the cab — he is mildly resentful about this and willing to talk to someone who will actually listen.
knowledge: He saw a man in a dark wool coat enter the tower's lower door at approximately 1:15 AM — the man was carrying a document satchel and did not look like a laborer.
He saw a second figure, lean and quick, working near the drill platform at around 2 AM — this figure left before dawn without speaking to anyone.

He has been watching the construction lights every night and can give approximate times for when the gas lanterns were extinguished and relit — a timeline that contradicts the crew's official night-watch log.
publicFace: A broad, soot-stained man of forty-five who has been idling a freight consist two blocks south of the tower for two nights running, waiting on a track clearance that keeps getting pushed back. He is bored, observant, and has nothing to lose by talking.
trustLogic: Responds well to directness and to anyone who acknowledges that his time is being wasted. Will not cooperate if he feels he is being treated as a suspect rather than a witness.
privateGoal: To get his consist moving and get home to his family in Naperville — he has no stake in the tower project except as a source of entertainment during a long delay.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Leans against the locomotive and talks freely — he has been wanting to tell someone.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not appear voluntarily before the justice of the peace without a formal subpoena — he is not afraid, he simply has a consist to move.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Crosses his arms and says he will need to hear from the railroad bull before he gives a sworn statement.
